title: brassica oleracea subsp. botrytis
intro: Brassica oleracea subsp. botrytis, commonly known as cauliflower, is a cool-season annual plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. The medicinal properties of cauliflower include its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, making it beneficial for reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Cauliflower is also rich in vitamins C and K, and fiber, providing several health benefits such as supporting cardiovascular health, reducing cancer risk, and promoting digestive regularity. Cauliflower can be prepared in various herbal forms, including fresh, frozen, or dried, and is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
